mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 9.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when UNRATE > 9.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts fall below 600k units, indicating joint demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors that predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month below 100.0 signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 50k new jobs per month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating joint labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 50k AND UNRATE rising > 0.2pp
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 3% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative despite inflation, supporting equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 1% annualized while retail sales decline month-over-month, signaling broad-based demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized AND RSAFS declining
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 101.0 while payroll employment grows above 150k per month, indicating joint supply and labor expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion regime with strong labor growth
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.
